name: Amalgamated Bank Of Chicago
description_short: ABOC is a Chicago-based bank with over 100 years of history serving unions, their members, and local businesses through commercial, personal, and corporate trust services.
description_long:
Amalgamated Bank of Chicago (ABOC) is a full-service bank with a century-long heritage focused on union banking and community lending. The bank rebranded in July 2024 to reflect a digital-first philosophy while maintaining its core commitment to responsible banking practices. ABOC operates as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, serving the unique financial needs of America's unions and their members.

ABOC offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal banking, commercial financing, union banking solutions, and extensive corporate trust services. Their personal services feature state-of-the-art online banking for managing finances remotely. On the commercial side, they provide customized financing solutions tailored to business needs. Their corporate trust department specializes in municipal debt administration, bond trustee services, escrow management, and related fiduciary products serving both tax-exempt and taxable debt issues.

What distinguishes ABOC is its Midwest-based corporate trust team with administrators averaging over 20 years of municipal trust experience. Unlike larger competitors, they consolidate trust administration, operations, compliance, trade settlement, wire transfers, and legal counsel in a single location, enabling faster response times and personalized service. Their structure is deliberately lean to avoid the multiple layers and bureaucracy of national banks, allowing them to tailor services to client-specific needs and maintain high attention to detail.

ABOC appears well-suited for unions, their members, small-to-mid-sized businesses, and municipal bond issuers seeking personalized, specialized service. However, as a regional bank with specialized focus, they may have limited product breadth compared to national institutions, and their services outside union and municipal trust sectors are not extensively detailed on their website.
services: Bond registrar and paying agent services
Municipal and corporate escrow agent services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Over 100 years of established history and trust in union banking
Corporate trust team with administrators averaging 20+ years of municipal debt experience
All trust services consolidated in single location for faster response times and coordination
In-house legal review and counsel for cohesive corporate trust experience
State-of-the-art online banking platform with free online statement viewing and downloads
Specialized expertise in municipal debt administration vs. generic corporate debt services
cons: Regional bank with limited geographic footprint compared to national banks
Primary focus on union and municipal sectors may mean limited product offerings for general consumers
Website lacks detailed information about personal banking products, rates, fees, or account minimums
No information about mobile banking app capabilities or features
Limited transparency on commercial financing terms, rates, or eligibility requirements

contact.address: 30 N La Salle St, Chicago, IL 60602

best_for: Union members and organizations seeking banking tailored to union financial needs
Municipal bond issuers and municipalities requiring specialized corporate trust services
Small-to-mid-sized businesses in the Midwest seeking customized commercial financing
Organizations needing professional municipal escrow or bond trustee administration
diagnosis: ABOC is best for unions, their members, municipalities, and regional businesses seeking personalized banking and specialized municipal bond services from a bank with deep historical expertise in these sectors. Primary caveat: As a regional bank heavily focused on union and municipal trust services, ABOC may offer limited product breadth and features compared to national banks, and detailed information about personal banking products and rates is not readily available on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 30 N La Salle St, Chicago, IL 60602
